question.text: Meera planned to save Rs.5,322 per month for retirement but could only save Rs.4,000 per month. If her target corpus was Rs.1,00,00,000 (1 crore), her actual corpus will be approximately:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** She saves 4,000/5,322 = 75.16% of the required amount. Actual corpus = 75.16% x Rs.1,00,00,000 = approximately Rs.75,00,000. **The Trap:** The textbook presents this as a proportional relationship for simplicity. **Cross-Exam Context:** Under-saving is one of the most common risks in the accumulation stage. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Set up automatic step-up SIPs that increase by 5-10% annually with salary increments.
